description:
You are acting as a senior cloud-migration architect.  
    A legacy configuration file already exists at  
    /home/user/cloud/services.ini

    Goal  
    -----  
    1. Read the [production] section of /home/user/cloud/services.ini.  
    2. Extract only the *values* (the hostnames that appear to the right of the equal sign).  
    3. Sort those hostnames in strict ASCII (byte) order, ascending.  
    4. Write the result to a brand-new file called  
       /home/user/cloud/production_services.txt  

    Output format requirements for production_services.txt  
    -------------------------------------------------------  
    • Each hostname must appear on its own line.  
    • No leading or trailing whitespace on any line.  
    • Lines must be terminated with a single UNIX newline character (␊).  
    • The file must contain nothing except those hostnames.  
    • Example of a correctly formatted file (line numbers shown only for clarity):  
         1 prod-api.internal  
         2 prod-cache.internal  
         3 prod-db.internal  
         4 prod-search.internal  
         5 prod-worker.internal  

    Verification artefact  
    ---------------------  
    To allow automated grading, also create /home/user/cloud/extract.log that contains:  
    • On its first (and only) line, reproduce verbatim the single terminal command you used to generate production_services.txt.  
      (Do *not* include the shell prompt or any additional commentary—just the command itself.)  
    • The log file must end with a UNIX newline.  

    Constraints  
    -----------  
    • Achieve everything with exactly one shell command (it may use pipes and redirects but must be typed and executed as a single command line).  
    • You do not need elevated privileges; stay within /home/user.  

    When you are finished, the two new files should exist with the precise contents described above.
truth:
Pre-existing filesystem state  
    -----------------------------  
    1. Directory /home/user/cloud already exists with rwx permissions for user.  
    2. File /home/user/cloud/services.ini is present with the following exact content (UNIX newlines, no trailing spaces):  
        [development]
        db=dev-db.internal
        cache=dev-cache.internal
        api=dev-api.internal

        [staging]
        db=stage-db.internal
        cache=stage-cache.internal
        api=stage-api.internal

        [production]
        db=prod-db.internal
        cache=prod-cache.internal
        api=prod-api.internal
        search=prod-search.internal
        worker=prod-worker.internal

    Expected artefacts after successful completion  
    ----------------------------------------------  
    A. /home/user/cloud/production_services.txt with exactly:  
        prod-api.internal
        prod-cache.internal
        prod-db.internal
        prod-search.internal
        prod-worker.internal
    (file ends with \n)  

    B. /home/user/cloud/extract.log containing **one** line—the precise command issued by the agent (whatever it is), followed by \n.
